perturbation.original_text: 3. The Recipient shall hold the Information in confidence, and shall use reasonable efforts to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Information. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Recipient shall not disclose or divulge the Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, including licensees or customers anywhere in the world. The Recipient may not use the Information for any purpose other than the aforesaid without the prior written consent of a duly authorized representative of Disclosing Party. The Recipient may disclose the Information only to its officers, employees and independent contractors who are necessary for the purpose of evaluating such Information, and Recipient shall be responsible for any disclosure by them in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Recipient shall hold the Information in confidence, and shall use reasonable efforts to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Information. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Recipient shall not disclose or divulge the Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, including licensees or customers anywhere in the world, except in cases of legal obligation. The Recipient may not use the Information for any purpose other than the aforesaid without the prior written consent of a duly authorized representative of Disclosing Party. The Recipient may disclose the Information only to its officers, employees and independent contractors who are necessary for the purpose of evaluating such Information, and Recipient shall be responsible for any disclosure by them in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change allows disclosure in cases of 'legal obligation,' but this is undefined. This creates a contradiction because section 3 otherwise strictly forbids disclosure and the vague term of legal obligation could permit breaches of confidence, leading to disputes about what constitutes such an obligation and under what circumstances disclosure is justified. Section 7 refers to violations of the agreement, and this alteration introduces unclarity to what constitutes a violation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereby acknowledge and agree that in the event of any violation or a threatened violation of this Agreement by the Recipient, the Disclosing Party shall be authorized and entitled to seek from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary and permanent injunctive relief. This Agreement shall be governed by the law of the United States of America and the State of New Jersey without regard to conflicts of laws principles. Sections 3-7 shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3 strictly forbids disclosure of information. The changed text adds an exception for 'legal obligation,' which is undefined. This creates a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text because the contradicted text allows Disclosing Party to seek injunctive relief for 'any violation' of the agreement. By adding the 'legal obligation' exception, the definition of what constitutes a violation is made ambiguous, creating a strong conflict with the contradicted text and uncertainty about what actions would trigger injunctive relief. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because the conflicting obligations create uncertainty about contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The parties hereby acknowledge and agree that in the event of any violation or a threatened violation of this Agreement by the Recipient, the Disclosing Party shall be authorized and entitled to seek from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary and permanent injunctive relief. This Agreement shall be governed by the law of the United States of America and the State of New Jersey without regard to conflicts of laws principles. Sections 3-7 shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The parties hereby acknowledge and agree that in the event of any violation or a threatened violation of this Agreement by the Recipient, the Disclosing Party shall be authorized and entitled to seek from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary injunctive relief, but not permanent injunctive relief. This Agreement shall be governed by the law of the United States of America and the State of New Jersey without regard to conflicts of laws principles. Sections 3-7 shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Disclosing Party is authorized to seek preliminary injunctive relief but not permanent injunctive relief in the event of a violation. This contradicts the original agreement, which allows for both preliminary and permanent injunctive relief. This change introduces ambiguity, undermining the enforceability of the agreement and inviting dispute over the remedies available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereby acknowledge and agree that in the event of any violation or a threatened violation of this Agreement by the Recipient, the Disclosing Party shall be authorized and entitled to seek from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary injunctive relief, but not permanent injunctive relief. This Agreement shall be governed by the law of the United States of America and the State of New Jersey without regard to conflicts of laws principles. Sections 3-7 shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 7 to state that the Disclosing Party can seek preliminary injunctive relief but not permanent injunctive relief. The contradicted text also refers to section 7 and restates the modified text. This creates a direct conflict within the document because the original agreement allowed for both preliminary and permanent injunctive relief. The change removes the option of permanent injunctive relief, creating an inconsistency regarding the remedies available, and directly conflicts with what was initially agreed upon. The contradiction is clear and strong, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Upon completion of the aforesaid evaluation and in the absence of further agreement of the parties, the Recipient shall cease all use and make no further use of the Information. At Disclosing Party's request, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all Information disclosed by Disclosing Party and shall retain no copies.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Upon completion of the aforesaid evaluation and in the absence of further agreement of the parties, the Recipient shall cease all use and make no further use of the Information, except as required by law. At Disclosing Party's request, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all Information disclosed by Disclosing Party and shall retain no copies, unless otherwise compelled by legal obligation.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by adding 'except as required by law' and 'unless otherwise compelled by legal obligation.' This contradicts the original agreement, which states that the recipient shall cease all use of the information. By including exceptions, uncertainty is introduced about when information can be used or retained, especially considering Sections 3 & 7 which discuss the requirements of confidentiality and recourse for violation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall hold the Information in confidence, and shall use reasonable efforts to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Information. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Recipient shall not disclose or divulge the Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, including licensees or customers anywhere in the world. The Recipient may not use the Information for any purpose other than the aforesaid without the prior written consent of a duly authorized representative of Disclosing Party. The Recipient may disclose the Information only to its officers, employees and independent contractors who are necessary for the purpose of evaluating such Information, and Recipient shall be responsible for any disclosure by them in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 introduces an exception ('except as required by law' and 'unless otherwise compelled by legal obligation') to the original obligation to cease all use and return or destroy all Information. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which mandates that the Recipient hold the Information in confidence, prevent unauthorized use, and not disclose it to any third party, except as expressly provided in the Agreement. The addition of the legal exception in Section 6 creates uncertainty about when information can be used or retained, conflicting with the strict confidentiality requirements outlined in the Contradicted Text. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 1. StrikeForce and Company are interested in disclosing to each other certain information relating to their respective business plans and proprietary technology (hereinafter "Information") for the purposes of evaluation and consultation. For the purposes of this Agreement, each party shall be in the position of "Disclosing Party" for the Information it discloses to the other party, and each party shall be in the position of "Recipient" for the Information it receives from the other party. Such Information is a commercial asset of considerable value to Disclosing Party, and Disclosing Party is willing to disclose such Information only under the terms and conditions set forth below. This Agreement, when signed by authorized representatives of each party, will confirm that the Recipient is willing to receive such Information of Disclosing Party subject to the following terms and conditions, which the parties intend to be legally binding.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. StrikeForce and Company are interested in disclosing to each other certain information relating to their respective business plans and proprietary technology (hereinafter "Information") for the purposes of evaluation, potential investment, and possible acquisition. For the purposes of this Agreement, each party shall be in the position of "Disclosing Party" for the Information it discloses to the other party, and each party shall be in the position of "Recipient" for the Information it receives from the other party. Such Information is a commercial asset of considerable value to Disclosing Party, and Disclosing Party is willing to disclose such Information only under the terms and conditions set forth below. This Agreement, when signed by authorized representatives of each party, will confirm that the Recipient is willing to receive such Information of Disclosing Party subject to the following terms and conditions, which the parties intend to be legally binding.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the purpose of the information disclosure from 'evaluation and consultation' to 'evaluation, potential investment, and possible acquisition.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement's restrictions (sections 3 & 4) are designed for simple evaluation and consultation only. This makes them potentially insufficient or inappropriate for potential investment or acquisition scenarios, leading to disputes about what is acceptable use of the information, and is problematic with section 6, regarding the cessation of use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon completion of the aforesaid evaluation and in the absence of further agreement of the parties, the Recipient shall cease all use and make no further use of the Information. At Disclosing Party's request, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all Information disclosed by Disclosing Party and shall retain no copies.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the purpose of information disclosure as 'evaluation and consultation,' while the changed text broadens it to include 'evaluation, potential investment, and possible acquisition.' The contradicted text states that 'upon completion of the aforesaid evaluation and in the absence of further agreement of the parties, the Recipient shall cease all use and make no further use of the Information.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the original agreement, and specifically the cessation of use in the contradicted section, is designed for the limited scope of 'evaluation and consultation.' Introducing 'potential investment and possible acquisition' necessitates different considerations and potentially longer-term use of the information, making the original cessation clause inappropriate and contradictory, thus aligning with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
